title: esops-doctor rule catalog
description: Top-level shape of a rule YAML file consumed by the embedded catalog, --rules-dir, and the user rules.d directory. A single file may declare one or more checks under the `checks:` sequence.

id.description: Stable rule identifier. Referenced verbatim by waivers, --rule-id, severity overrides.

name.description: Human-readable rule name (one short phrase, sentence-cased).

category.description: Grouping label such as resource_sanity, security, lifecycle, mappings, hygiene.

description.description: Multi-line description shown by `esops-doctor explain` and rendered in `docs rules`.

probe.description: Registered probe name; resolves through internal/probes at scan time.

condition.description: CEL expression returning bool. Rule passes when the expression evaluates to true.

count_expression.description: Optional CEL expression returning int; substituted into the message's {{count}} placeholder.

message.description: Failure message template. Supports {{count}} when count_expression is set or len(self) is meaningful.

remediation.description: How an operator fixes the finding.

command.description: Free-form instruction or shell snippet that fixes the finding.

esops_commands.description: Concrete `esops` subcommands surfaced alongside the finding. Each entry must start with `esops `.

tags.description: Free-form labels for filtering. `framework:cis` / `framework:soc2` / `framework:pci` mark compliance-relevant rules.

dialects.description: Cluster dialects the rule applies to. A rule whose declared dialect doesn't match the probed cluster is reported as skipped, not silently dropped.

affected_versions.description: Version markers the rule applies to (e.g. "7.x", "8.x"). Informational; the engine does not gate evaluation on them.

deprecated_aliases.description: Old rule IDs preserved for one minor version after a rename. Waivers and --rule-id still resolve through these.
